tools.name: look_up_incident_records
tools.title: Look up incident records
tools.description: Lookup ServiceNow Incident records using structured parameters instead of encoded queries.  This tool provides intelligent querying of ServiceNow tables by parsing natural language  and converting it to specific parameter values that ServiceNow can understand.  FIELD MAPPINGS AND VALUES:  Priority Mapping Instructions: - If user query mentions 'critical' priority or 'highest' priority or something similar, pass '1' or 'Critical' - If user query mentions 'high' priority or 'important' or something similar, pass '2' or 'High' - If user query mentions 'moderate' or 'medium' priority or something similar, pass '3' or 'Moderate' - If user query mentions 'low' priority or 'minor' or something similar, pass '4' or 'Low' - If user query mentions 'planning' priority or something similar, pass '5' or 'Planning'  Status Mapping Instructions: - If user query mentions 'new', 'open', 'not started', or something similar pass '1' or 'New' - If user query mentions 'in progress', 'active', 'working', 'ongoing' or something similar, pass '2' or 'In Progress' - If user query mentions 'on hold', 'waiting', 'pending' or something similar, pass '3' or 'On Hold' - If user query mentions 'resolved', 'fixed', 'completed' or something similar, pass '6' or 'Resolved' - If user query mentions 'closed', 'done', 'finished' or something similar, pass '7' or 'Closed' - If user query mentions 'cancelled', 'terminated' or something similar, 'aborted', pass '8' or 'Cancelled'  Date Parsing Instructions: CURRENT DATE: 2025-07-24 (use this as reference for all relative date calculations)  Convert natural language date expressions to YYYY-MM-DD format: - 'today' → '2025-07-24' - 'yesterday' → '2025-07-23'  - 'last week' → '2025-07-17' (7 days ago) - 'last month' → '2025-06-24' (30 days ago) - '3 days ago' → '2025-07-21' - 'last Monday' → '2025-07-21' (most recent Monday before today) - 'last Tuesday' → '2025-07-22' (most recent Tuesday before today) - 'last Friday' → '2025-07-18' (most recent Friday before today) - 'this Monday' → '2025-07-21' (Monday of current week) - 'beginning of this month' → '2025-07-01' - 'end of last month' → '2025-06-30' - 'since Monday' → calculate based on current week - 'this week' → range from Monday 2025-07-21 to today  For specific dates, convert to YYYY-MM-DD: - 'July 15' → '2025-07-15' (assume current year if not specified) - '15th July 2024' → '2024-07-15' - '2024-01-15' → '2024-01-15' (keep as-is)  For date ranges: - Start dates: Use date with 00:00:00 time - End dates: Use date with 23:59:59 time - Single dates: Use 00:00:00 for start, 23:59:59 for end when creating ranges  Special handling for 'ON' specific date queries: When user asks for events 'on' a specific date (e.g., 'closed on last Monday'): - Set both start and end parameters to the same date - Example: 'closed on last Monday' → closed_at_start='2025-07-21', closed_at_end='2025-07-21' - This creates a date range covering the entire day (00:00:00 to 23:59:59)  User References: - Use display names: 'John Smith', 'Ashish Singh' - Or partial names: 'Ashish', 'Smith'  Sort By Instructions: - Use ServiceNow field names: 'priority', 'state', 'sys_created_on', 'sys_updated_on', 'number' - Can specify direction with field name: 'priority DESC', 'sys_created_on ASC' - If no direction specified, defaults to ASC (ascending) - Common sort fields: 'priority' (1=Critical to 5=Planning), 'sys_created_on' (newest/oldest first), 'number' (incident number)  PARSING EXAMPLES:  Query: 'Give me all high priority incidents closed by Ashish in the last week' Parse to: - table: 'incident' - priority: '2' (High) - closed_by: 'Ashish' - closed_at_start: '2025-07-17' (convert 'last week' using current date 2025-07-24) - status: '7' (Closed)  Query: 'Show me critical incidents assigned to John Smith that are still open' Parse to: - table: 'incident' - priority: '1' (Critical) - assigned_to: 'John Smith' - status: '1' (New/Open)  Query: 'Get top 10 incidents sorted by priority' Parse to: - table: 'incident' - limit: 10 - sortby: 'priority'  Query: 'Show me recent incidents sorted by creation date descending' Parse to: - table: 'incident' - sortby: 'sys_created_on DESC'  Query: 'List incidents assigned to me, ordered by priority then by creation date' Parse to: - table: 'incident' - assigned_to: 'me' (or actual user name) - sortby: 'priority,sys_created_on'  Query: 'How many incidents were closed on last Monday' Parse to: - table: 'incident' - status: '7' (Closed) - closed_at_start: '2025-07-21' (convert 'last Monday' using current date 2025-07-24) - closed_at_end: '2025-07-21' (same date for 'on' queries to cover entire day)

assigned_to.description: Servicenow user the Incident is assigned to

limit.description: Number of records to be returned

closed_by.description: Servicenow user the Incident was closed by

tools.name: get_recent_m365_roadmaps
tools.description:
Retrieves M365 roadmap posts from Microsoft Release Communications with optional filtering and search capabilities.
Returns up to 50 roadmap items per request with truncated descriptions to manage AI context window.
Use GetM365RoadmapByIdAsync to get full details (including complete description) for specific items.
If results are empty or insufficient, ask user for more specific filter criteria.

RESULT EXPLANATION REQUIREMENT:
When returning filtered results:
- Order results from most recent to oldest based on the relevant date field used for filtering.
- Briefly state which date field was used:
- Availability dates (GA/preview): 'Filtered by feature release timing (when features become available to customers)'
- Publication dates (created/modified): 'Filtered by post publication date (when posts were published to roadmap)'
Example: 'Here are M365 features with GA in February 2026 (filtered by general availability date)...'

filter.description:
OData filter expression for precise querying.

BASIC FILTERS:
• Products: products/any(p: p eq 'Microsoft Teams') or products/any(p: p eq 'SharePoint')
• Status: status eq 'Launched' | 'Rolling out' | 'In development' | null
• Release Rings (also called Release phases): 
  releaseRings/any(r: r eq 'Current Channel') or releaseRings/any(r: r eq 'General Availability') or 
  releaseRings/any(r: r eq 'Preview') or releaseRings/any(r: r eq 'Targeted Release') or 
  releaseRings/any(r: r eq 'Targeted Release (Entire Organization)') or releaseRings/any(r: r eq 'Targeted Release (Select People)')
• Platforms: 
  platforms/any(pl: pl eq 'Android') or platforms/any(pl: pl eq 'Desktop') or platforms/any(pl: pl eq 'Developer') or 
  platforms/any(pl: pl eq 'Education') or platforms/any(pl: pl eq 'iOS') or platforms/any(pl: pl eq 'Linux') or 
  platforms/any(pl: pl eq 'Mac') or platforms/any(pl: pl eq 'Mobile') or platforms/any(pl: pl eq 'Teams and Surface Devices') or 
  platforms/any(pl: pl eq 'Web')
• Cloud Instances: 
  cloudInstances/any(ci: ci eq 'DoD') or cloudInstances/any(ci: ci eq 'GCC') or 
  cloudInstances/any(ci: ci eq 'GCC High') or cloudInstances/any(ci: ci eq 'Worldwide (Standard Multi-Tenant)')
• Availabilities (nested complex type with ring, year, month):
  availabilities/any(a: a/ring eq 'General Availability' and a/year eq 2026)
  availabilities/any(a: a/ring eq 'General Availability' and a/month eq 'February' and a/year eq 2026)
  availabilities/any(a: a/ring eq 'Preview' and a/year ge 2025)
  Ring values: 'General Availability', 'Preview'
  Month values: 'January', 'February', ..., 'December'
• Title: Use the 'search' parameter instead of filter for title text matching


DATE FILTERING (choose based on user intent):

1. FEATURE AVAILABILITY DATES (DEFAULT - use when user asks about RELEASE TIMING):
   USE THIS FILTER WHEN user query contains words like: 'coming', 'rolling out', 'launching', 'released', 'upcoming', 'planned', 'available', 'GA dates', 'preview dates', 'release dates'
   Format: YYYY-MM (e.g., '2026-01' for January 2026)
   Meaning: WHEN THE FEATURE BECOMES AVAILABLE to customers (release timing)
   
   Examples:
   - generalAvailabilityDate eq '2026-02' (features with GA in February 2026)
   - previewAvailabilityDate ge '2026-01' (previews available in or after January 2026)
   - generalAvailabilityDate le '2026-06' (GA on or before June 2026)
   - (generalAvailabilityDate eq '2026-02' or previewAvailabilityDate eq '2026-02') (GA OR preview in February 2026)

2. ANNOUNCEMENT/PUBLICATION DATES (use ONLY when user explicitly asks about when a post was published or modified):
   USE THIS FILTER WHEN user query contains words like: 'published', 'updated', 'created', 'added', 'last week', 'modified', 'announcement date', 'post creation date', 'when was this posted', 'newly added', 'recently updated'
   Format: ISO 8601 DateTime (e.g., 2026-02-01T00:00:00Z)
   Meaning: WHEN THE POST WAS PUBLISHED to M365 Roadmap (not when feature becomes available)
   
   Examples:
   - created ge 2026-02-01T00:00:00Z and created le 2026-02-28T23:59:59Z (posts published to roadmap in February 2026)
   - created ge 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z (posts published on or after Jan 1, 2026)
   - modified le 2026-12-31T23:59:59Z (posts last updated on or before Dec 31, 2026)
   - created ge 2025-12-01T00:00:00Z and created le 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z (posts published in December 2025)

COMBINING FILTERS:
• Multiple criteria: products/any(p: p eq 'Microsoft Teams') and status eq 'Launched'
• Product + Status + Platform: products/any(p: p eq 'Microsoft Purview') and status eq 'In development' and platforms/any(pl: pl eq 'Web')
• Product + Availability date: products/any(p: p eq 'Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365)') and (generalAvailabilityDate eq '2026-02' or previewAvailabilityDate eq '2026-02')
• Product + Announcement date: products/any(p: p eq 'Microsoft Teams') and created ge 2026-02-01T00:00:00Z and created le 2026-02-28T23:59:59Z
• Cloud + Status: cloudInstances/any(ci: ci eq 'GCC High') and status eq 'Launched'
• Release Ring + Availability: releaseRings/any(r: r eq 'Preview') and previewAvailabilityDate ge '2025-01'
• Product + Availabilities: products/any(p: p eq 'Microsoft Teams') and availabilities/any(a: a/ring eq 'General Availability' and a/month eq 'March' and a/year eq 2026)

AVAILABLE FIELDS:
id, baseId, status, created, modified, generalAvailabilityDate, previewAvailabilityDate, products, platforms, releaseRings, cloudInstances, availabilities (nested: ring, year, month)

OUTPUT-ONLY FIELDS (not filterable, included in response):
title, description, moreInfoUrls

search.description: Text search within post titles only. Use for finding posts containing specific keywords or phrases.

skip.description: Number of items to skip for pagination. Each request returns up to 50 items. For page 1: skip=0 (default). For page 2: skip=50. For page 3: skip=100. The response includes Offset, Limit, TotalCount, and HasMore to help determine next page.

include_facets.description: When true, includes facet taxonomy counts in the response showing distribution of Products, Platforms, ReleaseRings, and CloudInstances. Useful for discovering available filter values or understanding data distribution.

tools.name: get_recent_azure_updates
tools.description:
Retrieves Azure update posts from Microsoft Release Communications with optional filtering and search capabilities.
Returns up to 50 Azure updates per request with truncated descriptions to manage AI context window.
Use GetAzureUpdateByIdAsync to get full details (including complete description) for specific items.
If results are empty or insufficient, ask user for more specific filter criteria.

RETIREMENT QUERIES:
When user asks about retirements, retiring services, or end-of-life:
- Filter by tag: tags/any(t: t eq 'Retirements')
- To filter by retirement date, use the availabilities collection: availabilities/any(a: a/ring eq 'Retirement' and a/month eq 'February' and a/year eq 2026)
- You can combine both: tags/any(t: t eq 'Retirements') and availabilities/any(a: a/ring eq 'Retirement' and a/month eq 'February' and a/year eq 2026)
- Example: 'Azure retirements in Feb 2026' → tags/any(t: t eq 'Retirements') and availabilities/any(a: a/ring eq 'Retirement' and a/month eq 'February' and a/year eq 2026)
- Present retirement timing from the availabilities array: look for entries where ring='Retirement' and show the year and month.

RESULT EXPLANATION REQUIREMENT:
When returning filtered results:
- Order results from most recent to oldest based on the relevant date field used for filtering.
- Briefly state which date field was used:
- Availability dates (GA/preview): 'Filtered by feature release timing (when features become available to customers)'
- Retirement dates: 'Filtered by retirement tag. Retirement dates shown from the availabilities array (ring, year, month).'
- Publication dates (created/modified): 'Filtered by post publication date (when posts were published to Azure Updates)'
Example: 'Here are Azure features with GA in February 2026 (filtered by general availability date)...'

filter.description:
OData filter expression for precise querying.

BASIC FILTERS:
• Tags: tags/any(t: t eq 'Features') or tags/any(t: t eq 'Retirements') or tags/any(t: t eq 'Compliance') or tags/any(t: t eq 'Management') or tags/any(t: t eq 'Microsoft Ignite') or tags/any(t: t eq 'Open Source') or tags/any(t: t eq 'Pricing & Offerings') or tags/any(t: t eq 'Regions & Datacenters') or tags/any(t: t eq 'Security') or tags/any(t: t eq 'Services')
• Products: products/any(p: p eq 'Azure Storage') or products/any(p: p eq 'Azure Machine Learning')
• Product Categories: productCategories/any(pc: pc eq 'Compute') or productCategories/any(pc: pc eq 'Databases') or productCategories/any(pc: pc eq 'Networking') or productCategories/any(pc: pc eq 'Security') or productCategories/any(pc: pc eq 'Storage') or productCategories/any(pc: pc eq 'Analytics') or productCategories/any(pc: pc eq 'Containers') or productCategories/any(pc: pc eq 'DevOps') or productCategories/any(pc: pc eq 'Management and governance') or productCategories/any(pc: pc eq 'Web')
• Status: status eq 'Launched' | 'In preview' | 'In development' | null
• Title: Use the 'search' parameter instead of filter for title text matching

DATE FILTERING (choose based on user intent):

1. FEATURE AVAILABILITY DATES (DEFAULT - use when user asks about RELEASE TIMING):
   USE THIS FILTER WHEN user query contains words like: 'coming', 'rolling out', 'launching', 'released', 'upcoming', 'planned', 'available', 'GA dates', 'preview dates', 'release dates'
   Format: YYYY-MM (e.g., '2026-01' for January 2026)
   Meaning: WHEN THE FEATURE BECOMES AVAILABLE to customers (release timing)
   
   Examples:
   - generalAvailabilityDate eq '2026-02' (features with GA in February 2026)
   - previewAvailabilityDate ge '2026-01' (previews available in or after January 2026)
   - privatePreviewAvailabilityDate le '2026-06' (private previews on or before June 2026)
   - (generalAvailabilityDate eq '2026-02' or previewAvailabilityDate eq '2026-02') (GA OR preview in February 2026)

2. ANNOUNCEMENT/PUBLICATION DATES (use ONLY when user explicitly asks about when a post was published or modified):
   USE THIS FILTER WHEN user query contains words like: 'published', 'updated', 'created', 'added', 'last week', 'modified', 'announcement date', 'post creation date', 'when was this posted', 'newly added', 'recently updated'
   Format: ISO 8601 DateTime (e.g., 2026-02-01T00:00:00Z)
   Meaning: WHEN THE POST WAS PUBLISHED to Azure Updates website (not when feature becomes available)
   
   Examples:
   - created ge 2026-02-01T00:00:00Z and created le 2026-02-28T23:59:59Z (posts published in February 2026)
   - created ge 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z (posts published on or after Jan 1, 2026)
   - modified le 2026-12-31T23:59:59Z (posts last updated on or before Dec 31, 2026)
   - created ge 2025-12-01T00:00:00Z and created le 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z (posts published in December 2025)

COMBINING FILTERS:
• Multiple criteria: tags/any(t: t eq 'Features') and status eq 'Launched'
• Product + Availability date: products/any(p: p eq 'Azure Storage') and (generalAvailabilityDate eq '2026-02' or previewAvailabilityDate eq '2026-02')
• Product + Announcement date: products/any(p: p eq 'Azure Storage') and created ge 2026-02-01T00:00:00Z and created le 2026-02-28T23:59:59Z
• Retirements in a specific month: tags/any(t: t eq 'Retirements') and availabilities/any(a: a/ring eq 'Retirement' and a/month eq 'February' and a/year eq 2026)
• Product Category + Status: productCategories/any(pc: pc eq 'Databases') and status eq 'Launched'
• Product + Tag + Availabilities: products/any(p: p eq 'Automation') and tags/any(t: t eq 'Retirements') and availabilities/any(a: a/ring eq 'Retirement' and a/month eq 'March' and a/year eq 2025)

AVAILABLE FIELDS:
id, baseId, status, created, modified, privatePreviewAvailabilityDate, previewAvailabilityDate, generalAvailabilityDate, tags, products, productCategories, availabilities (nested: ring, year, month)

OUTPUT-ONLY FIELDS (not filterable, included in response):
title, description

search.description: Text search within post titles only. Use for finding posts containing specific keywords or phrases.

skip.description: Number of items to skip for pagination. Each request returns up to 50 items. For page 1: skip=0 (default). For page 2: skip=50. For page 3: skip=100. The response includes Offset, Limit, TotalCount, and HasMore to help determine next page.

include_facets.description: When true, includes facet taxonomy counts in the response showing distribution of ProductCategories, Products, and Tags. Useful for discovering available filter values or understanding data distribution.

tools.name: ListEvents
tools.description: Retrieve a list of events for the user with a given criteria - start and end datetimes, title of the meeting, attendee emails etc. This returns only the master event for recurring meetings.

startDateTime.description: Start date/time in ISO 8601 format (e.g., '2026-01-15T09:00:00Z'). Value should be UTC or contain the offset for the time zone. Default : user'sCurrentTime.

endDateTime.description: End date/time in ISO 8601 format (e.g., '2026-01-15T17:00:00Z'). Value should be UTC or contain the offset for the time zone. Default : currentTime + 90 days.

attendeeEmails.description: List of Attendee email addresses to search by

timeZone.description: timezone for the event (e.g., 'Pacific Standard Time'). Default : current user's timezone.

select.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return

top.description: Maximum number of events to return (default: 150)

orderby.description: Property name to sort results by (e.g., 'displayName', 'jobTitle'). Meeting startTime is specified if not used.

tools.name: ListCalendarView
tools.description: Retrieve events from a user's calendar view with recurring events expanded into individual instances. Use this whenever you need to find user's meetings in general, or one meeting instance of a recurring event (not master series) occurring in a time window before modifying, updating, or canceling a meeting.

userIdentifier.description: User identifier - can be email, Entra ID (GUID), display name, or 'me' for current user. Defaults to 'me' if not specified.

startDateTime.description: Start date/time in ISO 8601 format (e.g., '2026-01-15T09:00:00'). Value should be UTC or contain the offset for the time zone. Default: current time of current user

endDateTime.description: End date/time in ISO 8601 format (e.g., '2026-01-15T17:00:00'). Value should be UTC or contain the offset for the time zone. Default: current time of current user + 15 days

timeZone.description: Time zone for the event (e.g., 'Pacific Standard Time'). Default : current user's timezone.

subject.description: Search term to filter events by subject/title. Use this to find specific meetings by name.

select.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return

top.description: Maximum number of events to return (default: 150)

orderby.description: Property name to sort results by (e.g., 'start/dateTime'). Default is 'start/dateTime'.

tools.name: CreateEvent
tools.description: Create a new calendar event. Can provide names or email addresses for attendees (names will be resolved to emails). If time is provided, schedules even if there are conflicts. Default duration is 30 minutes. All events automatically include a Teams meeting link. Supports recurring events.

attendeeEmails.description: List of email or userPrincipalNames of attendees.Please fetch the email addresses of the users before making this call.

timeZone.description: Time zone for the event (e.g., 'Pacific Standard Time'). Default is current user's timezone.

bodyContentType.description: Body content type: 'Text' or 'HTML'. Default is 'HTML'.

isOnlineMeeting.description: Whether to create as an online meeting. Defaults to true.

allowNewTimeProposals.description: Whether invitees can propose a new time. Defaults to true.

recurrence.description: Recurrence specification with format: {"pattern": {"type": "daily|weekly|absoluteMonthly|relativeMonthly|absoluteYearly|relativeYearly", "interval": 1, "daysOfWeek": ["monday","wednesday"], "dayOfMonth": 15, "month": 3, "index": "first|second|third|fourth|last", "firstDayOfWeek": "sunday"}, "range": {"type": "endDate|noEnd|numbered", "startDate": "2026-01-15", "endDate": "2026-12-31", "numberOfOccurrences": 10}}. Omit for one-time events.

showAs.description: Free/busy status to show: 'free', 'tentative', 'busy', 'oof', 'workingElsewhere', 'unknown'. Default is 'busy'.

responseRequested.description: Whether a response is requested from invitees. Default is true.

tools.name: UpdateEvent
tools.description: Update an existing calendar event. Can add or remove attendees by providing names or email addresses (names will be resolved to emails automatically). IMPORTANT: If updating the body of an online meeting, you MUST first retrieve the current event body, extract the 'Microsoft Teams meeting' section (join link, meeting ID, dial-in info), and append it to the new body if needed. Otherwise the Teams meeting links will be lost.

eventId.description: The ID of the event to update

startDateTime.description: Updated start date/time in ISO 8601 format

endDateTime.description: Updated end date/time in ISO 8601 format

timeZone.description: Updated Time zone for the event (e.g., 'Pacific Standard Time').

attendeesToAdd.description: List of attendee email addresses or names to add

attendeesToRemove.description: List of attendee email addresses or names to remove

recurrence.description: Updated recurrence specification with format: {"pattern": {"type": "daily|weekly|absoluteMonthly|relativeMonthly|absoluteYearly|relativeYearly", "interval": 1, "daysOfWeek": ["monday","wednesday"], "dayOfMonth": 15, "month": 3, "index": "first|second|third|fourth|last", "firstDayOfWeek": "sunday"}, "range": {"type": "endDate|noEnd|numbered", "startDate": "2026-01-15", "endDate": "2026-12-31", "numberOfOccurrences": 10}}. Omit to keep existing recurrence.

responseRequested.description: Updated whether a response is requested from invitees

tools.name: get_board_items_page
tools.description: Get all items from a monday.com board with pagination support and optional column values and item descriptions. Returns structured JSON with item details, creation/update timestamps, and pagination info. Use the 'nextCursor' parameter from the response to get the next page of results when 'has_more' is true. To retrieve an item's description (the rich-text body/details of a monday.com item), set 'includeItemDescription' to true — the response will include the item description's document blocks with their content, type, and id. Use this whenever the user asks about an item's description, body, details, or notes. [REQUIRED PRECONDITION]: Before using this tool, if new columns were added to the board or if you are not familiar with the board's structure (column IDs, column types, status labels, etc.), first use get_board_info to understand the board metadata. This is essential for constructing proper filters and knowing which columns are available. VIEW-BASED FILTERING: If the user refers to a board view by name (e.g. "show me items in the 'Overdue' view"), first call get_board_info to get the board's views, find the matching view by name, then extract its filter field and pass it as the filters argument here.

boardId.description: The id of the board to get items from

itemIds.description: The ids of the items to get. The count of items should be less than 100.

searchTerm.description:
The search term to use for the search.
    - Use this when: the user provides a vague, incomplete, or approximate search term (e.g., “marketing campaign”, “John’s task”, “budget-related”), and there isn’t a clear exact compare value for a specific field.
    - Do not use this when: the user specifies an exact value that maps directly to a column comparison (e.g., name contains "marketing campaign", status = "Done", priority = "High", owner = "Daniel"). In these cases, prefer structured compare filters.

limit.description: The number of items to get

cursor.description: The cursor to get the next page of items, use the nextCursor from the previous response. If the nextCursor was null, it means there are no more items to get

includeColumns.description:
Whether to include column values in the response.
PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION: Only set this to true when you actually need the column data. Excluding columns significantly reduces token usage and improves response latency. If you only need to count items, get item IDs/names, or check if items exist, keep this false.

includeSubItems.description: Whether to include sub items in the response. PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION: Only set this to true when you actually need the sub items data.

subItemLimit.description: The number of sub items to get per item. This is only used when includeSubItems is true.

filtersStringified.description:
The configuration of filters to apply on the items. Before sending the filters, use get_board_info tool to check "filteringGuidelines" key for filtering by the column.
JSON-stringified value for "filters". The value MUST be a valid JSON string. Expected JSON schema: {"anyOf":[{"not":{}},{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","properties":{"columnId":{"type":"string","description":"The id of the column to filter by"},"compareAttribute":{"type":"string","description":"The attribute to compare the value to. This is OPTIONAL property."},"compareValue":{"anyOf":[{"type":"string"},{"type":"number"},{"type":"boolean"},{"type":"array","items":{"anyOf":[{"type":"string"},{"type":"number"}]}}],"description":"The value to compare the attribute to. This can be a string or index value depending on the column type."},"operator":{"type":"string","enum":["any_of","between","contains_terms","contains_text","ends_with","greater_than","greater_than_or_equals","is_empty","is_not_empty","lower_than","lower_than_or_equal","not_any_of","not_contains_text","starts_with","within_the_last","within_the_next"],"default":"any_of","description":"The operator to use for the filter"}},"required":["columnId","compareValue"],"additionalProperties":false}}],"description":"The configuration of filters to apply on the items. Before sending the filters, use get_board_info tool to check \"filteringGuidelines\" key for filtering by the column."}

filtersOperator.description: The operator to use for the filters

columnIds.description: The ids of the item columns and subitem columns to get, can be used to reduce the response size when user asks for specific columns. Works only when includeColumns is true. If not provided, all columns will be returned

orderByStringified.description:
The columns to order by, will control the order of the items in the response
JSON-stringified value for "orderBy". The value MUST be a valid JSON string. tools.name: create_item
tools.description: Create a new item with provided values, create a subitem under a parent item, or duplicate an existing item and update it with new values. Use parentItemId when creating a subitem under an existing item. Use duplicateFromItemId when copying an existing item with modifications.[REQUIRED PRECONDITION]: Before using this tool, if new columns were added to the board or if you are not familiar with the board's structure (column IDs, column types, status labels, etc.), first use get_board_info to understand the board metadata. This is essential for constructing proper column values and knowing which columns are available.

boardId.description: The id of the board to which the new item will be added

name.description: The name of the new item to be created, must be relevant to the user's request

groupId.description: The id of the group id to which the new item will be added, if its not clearly specified, leave empty

columnValues.description: A string containing the new column values for the item following this structure: {\"column_id\": \"value\",... you can change multiple columns at once, note that for status column you must use nested value with 'label' as a key and for date column use 'date' as key} - example: "{\"text_column_id\":\"New text\", \"status_column_id\":{\"label\":\"Done\"}, \"date_column_id\":{\"date\":\"2023-05-25\"},\"dropdown_id\":\"value\", \"phone_id\":\"123-456-7890\", \"email_id\":\"test@example.com\"}"

parentItemId.description: The id of the parent item under which the new subitem will be created

duplicateFromItemId.description: The id of existing item to duplicate and update with new values (only provide when duplicating)

tools.name: create_update
tools.description: Create a new update (comment/post) on a monday.com item. Updates can be used to add comments, notes, or discussions to items. You can optionally mention users, teams, or boards in the update. You can also reply to an existing update by using the parentId parameter.

itemId.description: The id of the item to which the update will be added

body.description: The update text to be created. Do not use @ to mention users, use the mentionsList field instead. use html tags to format the text, dont use markdown.

mentionsList.description: Optional JSON array of mentions in the format: [{"id": "123", "type": "User"}, {"id": "456", "type": "Team"}]. Valid types are: User, Team, Board, Project

parentId.description: The ID of the update to reply to. Use this parameter when you want to reply on an existing update leave it empty if you want to create a new update

tools.name: get_updates
tools.description: Get updates (comments/posts) from a monday.com item or board. Specify objectId and objectType (Item or Board) to retrieve updates. For Board queries, you can filter by date range using fromDate and toDate (both required together, ISO8601 format). By default, Board queries return only board discussion; set includeItemUpdates to true to also include updates on individual items. Returns update text, creator info, timestamps, and optionally replies and assets.

objectId.description: The ID of the item or board to get updates from

objectType.description: Type of object for which objectId was provided

limit.description: Number of updates per page (default: 25, max: 100)

includeReplies.description: Include update replies in the response

includeAssets.description: Include file attachments in the response

fromDate.description: Start of date range filter (e.g. "2025-01-01" or "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z"). Must be used together with toDate. Only supported for Board objectType.

toDate.description: End of date range filter (e.g. "2025-06-01" or "2025-06-01T23:59:59Z"). Must be used together with fromDate. Only supported for Board objectType.

includeItemUpdates.description: When objectType is Board, also include updates on individual items. Defaults to false, returning only board discussion. Set to true to retrieve all updates on a board, including updates on individual items.

tools.name: get_board_activity
tools.description: Get board activity logs for a specified time range (defaults to last 30 days)

boardId.description: The id of the board to get activity for

fromDate.description: Start date for activity range (ISO8601DateTime format). Defaults to 30 days ago

toDate.description: End date for activity range (ISO8601DateTime format). Defaults to now

includeData.description: Whether to include the raw data payload for each activity entry. The data field contains the full before/after state of changes and can be very large. Only set to true when you need the detailed change data.

tools.name: get_board_info
tools.description: Get comprehensive board information including metadata, structure, owners, and configuration. Also returns the board's views (e.g. table views, filter views) — each view includes its id, name, type, and a structured `filter` object.

boardId.description: The id of the board to get information for

tools.name: list_users_and_teams
tools.description:
Tool to fetch users and/or teams data. 

      MANDATORY BEST PRACTICES:
      1. ALWAYS use specific IDs or names when available
      2. If no ids available, use name search if possible (USERS ONLY)
      3. Use 'getMe: true' to get current user information
      4. AVOID broad queries (no parameters) - use only as last resort

      REQUIRED PARAMETER PRIORITY (use in this order):
      1. getMe - STANDALONE
      2. userIds
      3. name - STANDALONE (USERS ONLY, NOT for teams)
      4. teamIds + teamsOnly
      5. No parameters - LAST RESORT

      CRITICAL USAGE RULES:
      • userIds + teamIds requires explicit includeTeams: true flag
      • includeTeams: true fetches both users and teams, do not use this to fetch a specific user's teams rather fetch that user by id and you will get their team memberships.
      • name parameter is for USER search ONLY - it cannot be used to search for teams. Use teamIds to fetch specific teams.

userIds.description: Specific user IDs to fetch.[IMPORTANT] ALWAYS use when you have user IDs in context. PREFER over general search. RETURNS: user profiles including team memberships

teamIds.description:
Specific team IDs to fetch.[IMPORTANT] ALWAYS use when you have team IDs in context, NEVER fetch all teams if specific IDs are available.
      RETURNS: Team details with owners and optional member data.

name.description:
Name-based USER search ONLY. STANDALONE parameter - cannot be combined with others. PREFERRED method for finding users when you know names. Performs fuzzy matching.
      CRITICAL: This parameter searches for USERS ONLY, NOT teams. To search for teams, use teamIds parameter instead.

getMe.description:
[TOP PRIORITY] Use ALWAYS when requesting current user information. Examples of when it should be used: ["get my user" or "get my teams"].
      This parameter CONFLICTS with all others.

includeTeams.description: [AVOID] This fetches all teams in the account. To fetch a specific user's teams just fetch that user by id and you will get their team memberships.

teamsOnly.description: Fetch only teams, no users returned. Combine with includeTeamMembers for member details.

includeTeamMembers.description: Set to true only when you need additional member details for teams other than names and ids.

tools.name: change_item_column_values
tools.description: Change the column values of an item in a monday.com board

boardId.description: The ID of the board that contains the item to be updated

itemId.description: The ID of the item to be updated

columnValues.description: A string containing the new column values for the item following this structure: {\"column_id\": \"value\",... you can change multiple columns at once, note that for status column you must use nested value with 'label' as a key and for date column use 'date' as key} - example: "{\"text_column_id\":\"New text\", \"status_column_id\":{\"label\":\"Done\"}, \"date_column_id\":{\"date\":\"2023-05-25\"}, \"phone_id\":\"123-456-7890\", \"email_id\":\"test@example.com\"}"

tools.name: create_board
tools.description: Create a monday.com board

boardName.description: The name of the board to create

boardKind.description: The kind of board to create

boardDescription.description: The description of the board to create

workspaceId.description: The ID of the workspace to create the board in

tools.name: create_form
tools.description: Create a monday.com form. This will create a new form as well as a new board for which the form’s responses will be stored. The returned board_id is the ID of the board that was created while the returned formToken can be used for all future queries and mutations to continue editing the form.

destination_workspace_id.description: The workspace in which the form will be created in.

destination_folder_id.description: The folder in which the form will be created under.

destination_folder_name.description: The name of the folder in which the form will be created in.

board_owner_team_ids.description: Array of team IDs whose members will have owner permissions on the board in which the form will create items in.

board_subscriber_ids.description: Array of user IDs who will receive notifications about board activity for the board in which the form will create items in.

board_subscriber_teams_ids.description: Array of team IDs whose members will receive notifications about board activity for the board in which the form will create items in.

tools.name: update_form
tools.description:
Update a monday.com form. Handles the following form update actions that can only be done one at a time using the correct "action" input: 
    - update form's feature settings with the action "updateFeatures",
    - update form's appearance settings with the action "updateAppearance",
    - update form's accessibility settings with the action "updateAccessibility",
    - update form's title with the action "updateFormHeader",
    - update form's description with the action "updateFormHeader",
    - update form's question order with the action "updateQuestionOrder",
    - create a new form tag with the action "createTag",
    - delete a form tag with the action "deleteTag",
    - update a form tag with the action "updateTag",
    - set or update the form's password with the action "setFormPassword"
    - shorten form's url with the action "shortenFormUrl"
    - deactivate form with the action "deactivateForm"
    - reactivate form with the action "activateForm"

formToken.description: The unique identifier token for the form. Required for all form-specific operations.

action.description: The type of update action to perform on the form. Can be one of the following: activate, deactivate, shortenFormUrl, setFormPassword, createTag, deleteTag, updateTag, updateForm, updateAppearance, updateAccessibility, updateFeatures, updateQuestionOrder, updateFormHeader.

tools.name: get_form
tools.description: Get a monday.com form by its form token. Form tokens can be extracted from the form’s url. Given a form url, such as https://forms.monday.com/forms/abc123def456ghi789?r=use1, the token is the alphanumeric string that appears right after /forms/ and before the ?. In the example, the token is abc123def456ghi789.

formToken.description: The unique identifier token for the form. Required for all form-specific operations.

formToken.description: The unique identifier token for the form. Required for all form-specific operations.

questionId.description: The unique identifier for the question. Used to target specific questions within a form.

tools.name: read_docs
tools.description:
Get a collection of monday.com documents with their content as markdown. 

PAGINATION: 
- Default limit is 25 documents per page
- Use 'page' parameter to get additional pages (starts at 1)
- Check response for 'has_more_pages' to know if you should continue paginating
- If user asks for "all documents" and you get exactly 25 results, continue with page 2, 3, etc.

FILTERING: Provide a type value and array of ids:
- type: 'ids' for specific document IDs
- type: 'object_ids' for specific document object IDs  
- type: 'workspace_ids' for all docs in specific workspaces
- ids: array of ID strings (at least 1 required)

Examples:
- { type: 'ids', ids: ['123', '456'] }
- { type: 'object_ids', ids: ['123'] }
- { type: 'workspace_ids', ids: ['ws_101'] }

USAGE PATTERNS:
- For specific documents: use type 'ids' or 'object_ids' (A monday doc has two unique identifiers)
- For workspace exploration: use type 'workspace_ids' with pagination
- For large searches: start with page 1, then paginate if has_more_pages=true

type.description: Query type of ids parameter that is used query by: ids, object_ids, or workspace_ids

ids.description: Array of ID values for this query type (at least 1 required)

limit.description: Number of docs per page (default: 25). Affects pagination - if you get exactly this many results, there may be more pages.

order_by.description: The order in which to retrieve your docs. The default shows created_at with the newest docs listed first. This argument will not be applied if you query docs by specific ids.

page.description: The page number to return (starts at 1). Use this to paginate through large result sets. Check response for has_more_pages indicator.

Fetch complete JSON Schema 7 definitions for all available widget types in monday.com.
    
    This tool is essential before creating widgets as it provides:
    - Complete schema definitions for all supported widgets
    - Required and optional fields for each widget type
    - Data type specifications and validation rules
    - Detailed descriptions of widget capabilities
    
    Use this tool when you need to:

    This tool creates data visualization widgets that display information from monday.com boards:
    **Parent Containers:**
    - **DASHBOARD**: Place widget in a dashboard (most common use case)
    - **BOARD_VIEW**: Place widget in a specific board view
    
    **Critical Requirements:**
    1. **Schema Compliance**: Widget settings MUST conform to the JSON schema for the specific widget type
    2. **Use all_widgets_schema first**: Always fetch widget schemas before creating widgets
    3. **Validate settings**: Ensure all required fields are provided and data types match
    
    **Workflow:**
    1. Use 'all_widgets_schema' to get schema definitions
    2. Prepare widget settings according to the schema
    3. Use this tool to create the widget

tools.name: get_monday_dev_sprints_boards
tools.description:
Discover monday-dev sprints boards and their associated tasks boards in your account.

## Purpose:
Identifies and returns monday-dev sprints board IDs and tasks board IDs that you need to use with other monday-dev tools. 
This tool scans your recently used boards (up to 100) to find valid monday-dev sprint management boards.

## What it Returns:
- Pairs of sprints boards and their corresponding tasks boards
- Board IDs, names, and workspace information for each pair
- The bidirectional relationship between each sprints board and its tasks board

## Note:
Searches recently used boards (up to 100). If none found, ask user to provide board IDs manually.

tools.name: get_sprints_metadata
tools.description:
Get comprehensive sprint metadata from a monday-dev sprints board including:

## Data Retrieved:
A table of sprints with the following information:
- Sprint ID
- Sprint Name
- Sprint timeline (planned from/to dates)
- Sprint completion status (completed/in-progress/planned)
- Sprint start date (actual)
- Sprint end date (actual)
- Sprint activation status
- Sprint summary document object ID

## Parameters:
- **limit**: Number of sprints to retrieve (default: 25, max: 100)

Requires the Main Sprints board ID of the monday-dev containing your sprints.

sprintsBoardId.description: The ID of the monday-dev board containing the sprints

limit.description: The number of sprints to retrieve (default: 25, max: 100)

tools.name: get_sprint_summary
tools.description:
Get the complete summary and analysis of a sprint.

## Purpose:
Unlock deep insights into completed sprint performance. 

The sprint summary content including:
- **Scope Management**: Analysis of planned vs. unplanned tasks, scope creep
- **Velocity & Performance**: Individual velocity, task completion rates, workload distribution per team member
- **Task Distribution**: Breakdown of completed tasks by type (Feature, Bug, Tech Debt, Infrastructure, etc.)
- **AI Recommendations**: Action items, process improvements, retrospective focus areas

## Requirements:
- Sprint must be completed and must be created after 1/1/2025 

## Important Note:
When viewing the section "Completed by Assignee", you'll see user IDs in the format "@user-12345678". the 8 digits after the @is the user ID. To retrieve the actual owner names, use the list_users_and_teams tool with the user ID and set includeTeams=false for optimal performance.

sprintId.description: The ID of the sprint to get the summary for (e.g., "9123456789")

tools.name: ICMDataPlugin_QueryIncidents
tools.description: Query IcM incidents based on the provided parameters. Use this to find active outages, triage incidents, or review recent incident history. Usage guidance:- To find incidents owned by a specific team, set teamName (e.g., 'Telemetry'). To find incidents under a specific tenant/service, set tenantName (e.g., 'Skype', 'Skype Teams', 'Cosmic'). Combine teamName and tenantName to narrow results (e.g., teamName='Networking', tenantName='Cosmic').

startPeriod.description: The number defining the start of the time period, measured using units in periodTimeUnit (for example, startPeriod=90 with periodTimeUnit=Day means 90 days ago). Default value is 90.

endPeriod.description: The number defining the end of the time period, measured using units in periodTimeUnit (for example, endPeriod=0 with periodTimeUnit=Day means now). Default value is 0 (now).

periodTimeUnit.description: The unit of the time period specified by startPeriod and endPeriod. Default value is 'Day'.

showOutagesOnly.description: Whether to limit results to outages only

statusList.description: The statuses to include: ACTIVE, MITIGATED, RESOLVED, CORRELATING, HOLDING, NEW

teamName.description: The IcM team name.Do NOT provide a value unless the user explicitly states it. Leave null — the system resolves this automatically.

tenantName.description: The IcM tenant/service name.Do NOT provide a value unless the user explicitly states it. Leave null — the system resolves this automatically.

keywordList.description: Comma-separated keywords to search for in incident title and tags. Empty means no keyword filtering.

tools.name: ICMDataPlugin_QueryRetrospectives
tools.description: Query IcM retrospectives (postmortems) based on the user-provided parameters. (e.g. Show me retrospectives for Telemetry team -> set teamName to 'Telemetry')

startDay.description: The number of days to look back to define the date range start.

endDay.description: The number of days to look back to define the date range end. Default value is 0 (today).

statusList.description: The statuses to include: AIDRAFT, INPROGRESS, READYFORREVIEW, COMPLETED, ABANDONED

teamName.description: The IcM team name. Do NOT provide a value unless the user explicitly states it. Leave null — the system resolves this automatically.

tenantName.description: The IcM tenant/service name. Do NOT provide a value unless the user explicitly states it. Leave null — the system resolves this automatically.

keywordList.description: Comma-separated keywords to search for in incident title and tags. Empty means no keyword filtering.

startPeriod.description: The number defining the start of the time period. Default value is 12.

endPeriod.description: The number defining the end of the time period. Default value is 0 (now).

periodTimeUnit.description: The unit of the time period: day, hour. Default value is 'hour'.

teamId.description: The IcM team id (example: 28401). Optional, if none provided the user's team information will be used.

tools.name: ICMPlugin_GetsOnCallsByDescription
tools.description: Retrieves the contact information of engineers/managers who are on-call right now based on the given team description. On-call may also refer to DRI (Designated Responsible Individual), IM (Incident Manager). This is used to provide operations support for incidents, DO NOT use to find privacy or security champs. If the user is asking for an IM/Incident Manager, append 'Incident Manager' to the team descriptor if not specified. Examples: 1. Who is on call for Client Release team? -> Client Release. 2. Who is IM for Teams today -> Teams Incident Manager. 3. Get DRI for SKYPETEAMS\livesite-tooling -> SKYPETEAMS\livesite-tooling 4. Who is Teams Incident Manager -> Teams Incident Manager

teamDescriptor.description: A user-provided reference to an IcM team. This can be a full or partial team name or public ID (e.g., 'Performance', 'TenantName\TeamName').

tools.name: AgencyPlugin_CreateCodingAgentJob
tools.description:
Creates a job to have an AI coding agent make code changes to a repository and open a pull request.
The coding agent will analyze the prompt, make the necessary code changes, and create a pull request with the changes.
Use this when the user wants to:
- Create a pull request with code changes
- Fix a bug in the code (first get the bug details from ADO, then pass a detailed prompt)
- Upgrade or update packages/NuGet versions
- Refactor code
- Implement a new feature
- Make any automated code changes to a repository

organization.description: The Azure DevOps organization. Never prompt the user for this parameter. Supply null if unavailable.

project.description: The Azure DevOps project. Never prompt the user for this parameter. Supply null if unavailable.

prompt.description:
A detailed prompt describing what code changes to make.
Be specific and include all relevant context. For bug fixes, include the bug description and any relevant details.
Examples:
- 'Update the Semantic Kernel NuGet package from version 1.0.0 to 1.5.0'
- 'Fix the null reference exception in UserService.GetUserAsync by adding null checks'
- 'Refactor the authentication middleware to use the new token provider'

repositoryName.description:
The repository name where code changes will be made.
Do NOT invoke ANY tools to look up or validate repositories before calling this function.
Do NOT ask the user to confirm or choose a repository before calling this function.
Rules for picking the value (apply in order, use the FIRST one that applies):
1.

workItemId.description:
Work item ID the coding job is addressing. This parameter is OPTIONAL — pass null when there is no work item.
Do NOT ask the user to provide, confirm, or pick a work item ID before calling this function. If no work item is already in context, pass null.
Pass a numeric ID ONLY when the prompt was built from a specific work item (bug fix, task implementation, etc.) that you already have details for, so the work item can be linked to the created pull request. A work item merely mentioned earlier in the chat does not count — only pass an ID that came from actual work item data already retrieved.
Self-contained / ad-hoc code change requests (e.g. upgrade this package, add a killswitch, refactor this method) have no work item — pass null.

targetBranch.description:
Optional target branch for the agent's commits.
- Leave null for the standard create a new PR flow (agent works against the repository's default branch and a new PR is opened).
- Set to an existing PR's SOURCE branch when the user wants to add changes to that PR. No new PR will be created — the commits land on that branch and show up in the existing PR.
- Set to a specific branch name when the user explicitly named one.
Pass the bare branch name (e.g. users/alice/feature-x) or the fully qualified ref (e.g. refs/heads/users/alice/feature-x).

plugins.description: Optional plugins to attach to the run. Each item must have a name and a type ('Repo', 'Organization', or 'Company'). Use GetAvailablePlugins to discover valid names. Pass null to use the defaults.

agent.description: Optional agent override. Pass null to let the default coding agent run. Use GetAvailableAgents to discover valid names.

tools.name: AgencyPlugin_GetCodingAgentJobStatus
tools.description: Gets the status of an existing coding agent job. Use this to check if the code changes have been completed.

jobId.description: The job ID returned when the coding agent job was created

baseIteration.description: For 'list': The base iteration ID. Defaults to the latest base iteration.

status.description: For 'list': Filter threads by status. If not specified returns all statuses.

authorEmail.description: For 'list': Filter threads by the email of the thread author (first comment).

authorDisplayName.description: For 'list': Filter threads by the display name of the thread author (case-insensitive partial).

threadId.description: Required for 'list_comments'. The ID of the thread to retrieve comments for.

tools.name: repo_pull_request
tools.description: Read operations on Azure DevOps pull requests. Use 'action' to choose: 'get' [requires repositoryId, pullRequestId, project] — get a PR by ID; 'list' [requires repositoryId OR project] — list PRs with optional filters; 'list_by_commits' [requires project, repository, commits] — find PRs containing the given commits; 'get_changes' [requires repositoryId, pullRequestId] — get file changes and line-by-line diffs for a PR iteration.

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'get', 'list', 'list_by_commits', 'get_changes'.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

repositoryId.description: Required for 'get' and 'get_changes'; optional for 'list' (used as a filter scope). For 'list_by_commits' use the 'repository' parameter instead. The ID or name of the repository. When a name is used, the project parameter must also be provided.

repository.description: Required for 'list_by_commits'. The ID or name of the repository.

project.description: Required for 'get' and 'list_by_commits'. Optional for 'list' (scopes the search). Required for 'get_changes' and other actions when repositoryId is a repository name (not a GUID). Project ID or project name.

pullRequestId.description: Required for 'get' and 'get_changes'. The ID of the pull request to retrieve.

includeWorkItemRefs.description: For 'get': Whether to reference work items associated with the pull request.

includeLabels.description: For 'get': Whether to include a summary of labels in the response.

top.description: For 'list': The maximum number of pull requests to return. For 'get_changes': The maximum number of file changes to return (default 100).

skip.description: For 'list': The number of pull requests to skip. For 'get_changes': The number of file changes to skip for pagination.

status.description: For 'list': Filter pull requests by status. Allowed values: 'Abandoned', 'Active', 'All', 'Completed', 'NotSet'.

sourceRefName.description: For 'list': Filter pull requests from this source branch (e.g., 'refs/heads/feature-branch').

targetRefName.description: For 'list': Filter pull requests into this target branch (e.g., 'refs/heads/main').

createdByMe.description: For 'list': Filter pull requests created by the current user. Ignored if createdByUser is provided.

createdByUser.description: For 'list': Filter pull requests created by a specific user (provide email address). Takes precedence over createdByMe.

iAmReviewer.description: For 'list': Filter pull requests where the current user is a reviewer. Ignored if userIsReviewer is provided.

userIsReviewer.description: For 'list': Filter pull requests where a specific user is a reviewer (provide email address). Takes precedence over iAmReviewer.

commits.description: For 'list_by_commits': Array of commit IDs to query for.

iterationId.description: For 'get_changes': The iteration ID to get changes for. If not specified, gets changes for the latest iteration.

compareTo.description: For 'get_changes': Iteration ID to compare against. If specified, returns changes between the two iterations.

includeDiffs.description: For 'get_changes': Whether to include actual line-by-line diff content. Default is true. Set to false to get only file metadata.

includeLineContent.description: For 'get_changes': Whether to include actual line content from the changed files. Default is true. When true, fetches file content and includes the code lines that were added/removed/modified.

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'get_content', 'list_directory'.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

repositoryId.description: Required. The ID (GUID) or name of the repository. If you do not have this, call repo_repository action=list (or repo_repository action=get with a known name) first.

project.description: Project ID or project name. Required when repositoryId is a name.

path.description: For 'get_content' (required): Full file path. For 'list_directory' (optional, defaults to '/'): Directory path.

version.description: Version identifier — branch (e.g., 'main'), tag, or commit SHA. Defaults to the repository's default branch.

versionType.description: How to interpret 'version'. 'Branch', 'Commit', or 'Tag'. Defaults to 'Branch'.

recursive.description: For 'list_directory': Whether to list items recursively. Defaults to false.

recursionDepth.description: For 'list_directory': Maximum recursion depth (1-10). Only applies when recursive is true.

tools.name: pipelines_write
tools.description: Destructive pipeline operations. Use 'action' to choose: 'run_pipeline' (queue a new run), 'create_pipeline' (define a new YAML pipeline), 'update_build_stage' (cancel/retry/run a stage on an in-flight build). Marked destructiveHint=true so hosts can gate with HITL confirmation.

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'run_pipeline', 'create_pipeline', 'update_build_stage'.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

project.description: Project ID or name. Required for all actions.

pipelineVersion.description: For 'run_pipeline': Version of the pipeline to run. If not provided, the latest version will be used.

previewRun.description: For 'run_pipeline': If true, returns the final YAML document after parsing templates without creating a new run.

resources.description: For 'run_pipeline': Resources to use for the pipeline run, such as repositories and pipelines. To target a specific branch, pass repositories with a 'self' key and a refName, e.g. {"repositories": {"self": {"refName": "refs/heads/my-branch"}}}

stagesToSkip.description: For 'run_pipeline': A list of stages to skip.

templateParameters.description: For 'run_pipeline': Custom build parameters as key-value pairs.

yamlOverride.description: For 'run_pipeline': YAML override. Only valid when previewRun=true.

name.description: For 'create_pipeline': Name of the new pipeline.

yamlPath.description: For 'create_pipeline': Path to a YAML file that already exists on the default branch of the target repo - verify with repo_file action=get_content before calling.

repositoryType.description: For 'create_pipeline': Required. The type of repository where the pipeline's YAML file is located.

repositoryName.description: For 'create_pipeline': For GitHub: 'owner/repo' (e.g., 'octocat/Hello-World'). For Azure Repos: the repo display name.

folder.description: For 'create_pipeline': Folder path for the new pipeline. Defaults to '\\' if not specified.

repositoryId.description: For 'create_pipeline': REQUIRED for Azure Repos (must be a non-empty GUID - look up via repo_repository action=get). Omit for GitHub.

repositoryConnectionId.description: For 'create_pipeline': REQUIRED when repositoryType is 'gitHub' or 'gitHubEnterprise' (service connection GUID). Omit for Azure Repos.

stageName.description: For 'update_build_stage': Exact stage name from the build's timeline.

status.description: For 'update_build_stage': New status for the stage (Cancel, Retry, Run).

forceRetryAllJobs.description: For 'update_build_stage': Whether to force retry all jobs in the stage.

tools.name: pipelines_definition

'list' returns a trimmed page of definitions ({ items, continuationToken }) with optional filters — pass continuationToken back with the same queryOrder to fetch the next page; 'list_revisions' returns the revision history for one definition. For 'list', repositoryId accepts a repository name or GUID — names are auto-resolved for Azure Repos.

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'list', 'list_revisions'.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

project.description: Project ID or name. Required for both actions.

repositoryId.description: For 'list': Repository ID or name to filter build definitions. For Azure Repos (TfsGit), a repository name is auto-resolved to the GUID using the project parameter. For GitHub use OwnerName/Repository format and always pair with repositoryType.

repositoryType.description: For 'list': Required whenever repositoryId is set as a GUID or non-TfsGit format.

name.description: For 'list': Name of the build definition to filter.

path.description: For 'list': Path of the build definition to filter.

queryOrder.description: For 'list': Order in which build definitions are returned.

top.description: For 'list': Maximum number of build definitions to return.

continuationToken.description: For 'list': Token for continuing paged results. Must be paired with the same queryOrder used in the prior call.

minMetricsTimeInUtc.description: For 'list': Minimum metrics time to filter build definitions.

definitionIds.description: For 'list': Array of build definition IDs to filter. Must be a JSON array of integers, e.g. [123, 456].

builtAfter.description: For 'list': Return definitions that have builds after this date.

notBuiltAfter.description: For 'list': Return definitions that do not have builds after this date.

includeLatestBuilds.description: For 'list': Whether to include the latest builds for each definition.

taskIdFilter.description: For 'list': Task ID to filter build definitions.

processType.description: For 'list': Process type to filter build definitions.

yamlFilename.description: For 'list': YAML filename filter (e.g., 'azure-pipelines.yml'). Returns full property expansion implicitly - slightly heavier response.

definitionId.description: For 'list_revisions': ID of the build definition to get revisions for (> 0).

Distinct from pipelines_write action=run_pipeline (which queues a new run); this tool is read-only.

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'get', 'list'.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

project.description: Project ID or name. Required for both actions.

pipelineId.description: Numeric pipeline ID (> 0). This is the pipeline ID, distinct from a build definition ID - get it from pipelines_definition action=list's id field.

tools.name: wit_work_item_write
tools.description: Write operations on Azure DevOps work items. Use 'action' to choose: 'create' (create a new work item), 'update' (update fields on a work item), 'update_batch' (update multiple work items in one call), 'add_child' (create child work items under a parent).

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'create', 'update', 'update_batch', 'add_child'.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project. Required for 'create', 'add_child'; optional for 'update'; not used for 'update_batch'.

id.description: For 'update': The ID of the work item to update.

parentId.description: For 'add_child': The ID of the parent work item to create children under.

workItemType.description: For 'create', 'add_child': The work item type, e.g., 'Task', 'Bug', 'User Story'.

fields.description: For 'create': A JSON array of field name/value pairs to set on the new work item. Must be an array, even when setting a single field. Example: [{"name":"System.Title","value":"My bug"},{"name":"System.Tags","value":"urgent"}]. Do not pass this value as a JSON-encoded string or as a single object.

name.description: The name of the field, e.g., 'System.Title'.

format.description: The format of the field value. Use 'Markdown' for multiline HTML fields containing markdown content (Description, ReproSteps, AcceptanceCriteria, etc.) so AzDO renders them as markdown. Omit for plain-text fields (Title, Tags) and short single-line fields -- AzDO rejects the markdown hint on those with 'Operation of changing value type is not supported'. When omitted, the value is stored as-is and the field's native rendering applies.

updates.description: For 'update': A JSON array of field update operations to apply to the work item. Must be an array, even when updating a single field. Example: [{"op":"Replace","path":"/fields/System.Title","value":"Updated title"}]. Do not pass this value as a JSON-encoded string or as a single object.

op.description: The operation to perform on the field.

path.description: The path of the field to update, e.g., '/fields/System.Title'.

value.description: The new value for the field. This is required for 'Add' and 'Replace' operations, and should be omitted for 'Remove' operations.

format.description: The format of the field value. Use 'Markdown' for multiline HTML fields containing markdown content (Description, ReproSteps, AcceptanceCriteria, etc.) so AzDO renders them as markdown. Omit for plain-text fields (Title, Tags) and short single-line fields -- AzDO rejects the markdown hint on those with 'Operation of changing value type is not supported'. When omitted, the value is stored as-is and the field's native rendering applies.

batchUpdates.description: For 'update_batch': A JSON array of batch update operations. Each entry includes operation, work item ID, field path, and value. Must be an array. Example: [{"id":123,"op":"Replace","path":"/fields/System.State","value":"Active"}]. Do not pass this value as a JSON-encoded string or as a single object.

op.description: The operation to perform on the field.

id.description: The ID of the work item to update.

path.description: The path of the field to update, e.g., '/fields/System.Title'.

value.description: The new value for the field. This is required for 'Add' and 'Replace' operations, and should be omitted for 'Remove' operations.

format.description: The format of the field value. Use 'Markdown' for multiline HTML fields containing markdown content (Description, ReproSteps, AcceptanceCriteria, etc.) so AzDO renders them as markdown. Omit for plain-text fields (Title, Tags) and short single-line fields -- AzDO rejects the markdown hint on those with 'Operation of changing value type is not supported'. When omitted, the value is stored as-is and the field's native rendering applies.

items.description: For 'add_child': A JSON array of child work items to create. Must be an array, even when creating a single child. Example: [{"title":"Subtask 1","description":"..."}]. Do not pass this value as a JSON-encoded string or as a single object.

title.description: The title of the child work item.

description.description: The description of the child work item.

areaPath.description: Optional area path for the child work item.

iterationPath.description: Optional iteration path for the child work item.

format.description: The format of the description field value. Use 'Markdown' if the description contains markdown content; AzDO will render it as markdown. Omit if the description is plain text or HTML. Default is no format hint, which preserves the field's native rendering.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

tools.name: search_wiki
tools.description: Search Azure DevOps Wiki for a given search text. Each result includes both the raw git filesystem 'path' and a 'pagePath' that is directly usable with the 'wiki' tool's 'get_page' action.

searchText.description: Keywords to search for wiki pages

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

project.description: Filter by projects

includeFacets.description: Include facets in the search results

top.description: Maximum number of results to return

tools.name: wit_query_by_wiql
tools.description: Execute a WIQL (Work Item Query Language) query and return the matching work items.

wiql.description: The WIQL query string to execute, e.g., "SELECT [System.Id], [System.Title] FROM WorkItems WHERE [System.TeamProject] = @project"

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

team.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps team. If not provided, the default team context will be used.

timePrecision.description: Whether to include time precision in date fields. Defaults to false.

top.description: The maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 50. Maximum is 1000.

tools.name: work
tools.description: Read operations on Azure Boards iterations, team settings, and capacity. Use 'action' to choose: 'list_iterations' (project-wide iteration classification nodes; supports 'depth' and 'excludedIds'), 'list_team_iterations' (iterations assigned to a team; requires 'team'; optional 'timeframe'), 'get_team_settings' (team settings including default iteration, backlog iteration, default area path; requires 'team'), 'get_team_capacity' (capacity for a team in a specific iteration; requires 'team' + 'iterationId'), 'get_iteration_capacities' (capacities across all teams for a specific iteration; requires 'iterationId').

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'list_iterations', 'list_team_iterations', 'get_team_settings', 'get_team_capacity', 'get_iteration_capacities'.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required.

team.description: For 'list_team_iterations', 'get_team_settings', 'get_team_capacity': the name or ID of the Azure DevOps team. Required.

iterationId.description: For 'get_team_capacity', 'get_iteration_capacities': the iteration ID to look up. Required

timeframe.description: For 'list_team_iterations': the timeframe for which to retrieve iterations. Currently, only 'current' is supported.

depth.description: For 'list_iterations': depth of children to fetch.

excludedIds.description: For 'list_iterations': an optional array of iteration IDs, and their children, that should not be returned.

tools.name: repo_pull_request_write
tools.description: Write operations on Azure DevOps pull requests. Use 'action' to choose: 'create' [requires repositoryId, sourceRefName, targetRefName, title] — create a new pull request; 'update' [requires repositoryId, pullRequestId, project] — update fields on an existing pull request, including autocomplete; 'update_reviewers' [requires repositoryId, pullRequestId, reviewerIds, reviewerAction] — add or remove reviewers; 'vote' [requires repositoryId, pullRequestId, vote] — cast a vote as the current user.

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'create', 'update', 'update_reviewers', 'vote'.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

repositoryId.description: Required for all actions. The ID or name of the repository the pull request belongs to. When a name is used, the project parameter must also be provided.

project.description: Required for 'update'. Required for all other actions when repositoryId is a repository name (not a GUID). Project ID or project name.

pullRequestId.description: Required for 'update', 'update_reviewers', 'vote'. The ID of the pull request.

sourceRefName.description: Required for 'create'. The source branch name (e.g., 'refs/heads/feature-branch').

targetRefName.description: Required for 'create'. Optional for 'update' (only when changing target). The target branch name (e.g., 'refs/heads/main').

title.description: Required for 'create'. Optional for 'update' (only when changing title). The title of the pull request.

description.description: For 'create', 'update': The description of the pull request. Maximum 4000 characters; longer values will be rejected.

isDraft.description: For 'create' (default false) and 'update' (nullable): Whether the pull request is a draft.

workItems.description: For 'create': Work item IDs to associate with the pull request, space-separated.

forkSourceRepositoryId.description: For 'create': The ID of the fork repository the pull request originates from.

labels.description: For 'create' (add labels) and 'update' (replace labels): Array of label names.

status.description: For 'update': The new status of the pull request. 'Active' or 'Abandoned'.

autoComplete.description: For 'update': Set the pull request to autocomplete when requirements are met.

mergeStrategy.description: For 'update': Merge strategy to use when autocompleting. Defaults to 'NoFastForward'.

mergeCommitMessage.description: For 'update': Commit message to use when the pull request is completed via autocomplete.

deleteSourceBranch.description: For 'update': Whether to delete the source branch when the PR autocompletes.

transitionWorkItems.description: For 'update': Whether to transition associated work items when the PR autocompletes.

bypassReason.description: For 'update': Reason for bypassing branch policies when autocompleting (also enables bypass).

reviewerIds.description: Required for 'update_reviewers'. List of reviewer IDs or email addresses to add or remove.

reviewerAction.description: Required for 'update_reviewers'. 'add' or 'remove' the listed reviewers.

vote.description: Required for 'vote'. The vote to cast. Allowed: Approved, ApprovedWithSuggestions, NoVote, WaitingForAuthor, Rejected.

tools.name: pipelines_build
tools.description: Read operations on Azure Pipelines builds. Use 'action' to choose: 'list' (search builds with filters or fetch by IDs; response is { items, continuationToken } — pass continuationToken back with the same queryOrder to fetch the next page), 'get_status' (status, issues, and report metadata for one build), 'get_changes' (commits/work-items associated with one build; also returns { items, continuationToken }). For 'list', repositoryId accepts a repository name or GUID — names are auto-resolved for Azure Repos. Discovery flow: core_list_projects -> pipelines_definition action=list -> this tool. Never invent project names or build IDs - look them up.

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'list', 'get_status', 'get_changes'.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

project.description: Project ID or name. Must match a project the caller can access - call core_list_projects first if uncertain.

buildId.description: For 'get_status', 'get_changes': numeric build ID (> 0). Get from action=list - never pass 0/1/2 placeholders.

definitions.description: For 'list': Array of build definition IDs to filter builds.

queues.description: For 'list': Array of queue IDs to filter builds.

buildNumber.description: For 'list': Build number to filter builds.

minFinishTime.description: For 'list': Minimum finish time to filter builds.

maxFinishTime.description: For 'list': Maximum finish time to filter builds.

requestedFor.description: For 'list': User ID or name who requested the build.

reasonFilter.description: For 'list': Reason filter for the build (see BuildReason enum).

statusFilter.description: For 'list': Status filter. 'All' cannot combine with resultFilter; 'Completed' cannot combine with queryOrder=QueueTimeDescending.

resultFilter.description: For 'list': Result filter for the build (see BuildResult enum).

tagFilters.description: For 'list': Array of tags to filter builds.

properties.description: For 'list': Array of property names to include in the results.

top.description: For 'list': Maximum number of builds to return.

continuationToken.description: For 'list': Token for continuing paged results. Must be paired with the same queryOrder used in the prior call.

maxBuildsPerDefinition.description: For 'list': Maximum number of builds per definition.

deletedFilter.description: For 'list': Filter for deleted builds (see QueryDeletedOption enum).

queryOrder.description: For 'list': Order in which builds are returned.

branchName.description: For 'list': Branch name to filter builds.

buildIds.description: For 'list': Array of build IDs to retrieve. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with all other filter parameters (definitions, queues, branchName, statusFilter, resultFilter, tagFilters, ...). Must be a JSON array of integers, e.g. [123, 456].

repositoryId.description: For 'list': Repository ID or name to filter builds. For Azure Repos (TfsGit), a repository name is auto-resolved to the GUID using the project parameter. For GitHub use OwnerName/Repository format and always pair with repositoryType. NOTE: when a repository filter is supplied the underlying SDK overload does not support continuation tokens, so the response will contain a single page (continuationToken=null) regardless of 'top'.

repositoryType.description: For 'list': Required whenever repositoryId is set as a GUID or non-TfsGit format.

changesTop.description: For 'get_changes': Number of changes to retrieve, defaults to 100.

includeSourceChange.description: For 'get_changes': Whether to include source changes in the results, defaults to false.

tools.name: wit_backlog
tools.description: Read operations on Azure DevOps backlogs. Use 'action' to choose: 'list' (backlog levels for a team), 'list_work_items' (work items in a specific backlog level).

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'list', 'list_work_items'.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

team.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps team.

backlogId.description: For 'list_work_items': The ID of the backlog category to retrieve work items from.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

tools.name: repo_create_branch
tools.description: Create a new branch in the repository.

repositoryId.description: The ID or name of the repository where the branch will be created. When using a repository name instead of a GUID, the project parameter must also be provided.

branchName.description: The name of the new branch to create, e.g., 'feature-branch'.

sourceBranchName.description: The name of the source branch to create the new branch from. Defaults to 'main'.

sourceCommitId.description: The commit ID to create the branch from. If not provided, uses the latest commit of the source branch.

project.description: Project ID or project name. Required when repositoryId is a repository name instead of a GUID.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

tools.name: search_workitem
tools.description: Get Azure DevOps Work Item search results for a given search text
