orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

project.description: Filter by projects

includeFacets.description: Include facets in the search results

skip.description: Number of results to skip for pagination

continuationToken.description: Continuation token for pagination. Used to fetch the next set of results if available.

tools.name: wit_work_item_link_write
tools.description: Write operations on work item links. Use 'action' to choose: 'link' (link two work items), 'unlink' (remove links from a work item), 'link_to_pull_request' (link a work item to a pull request), 'add_artifact_link' (add a repository/branch/commit/build artifact link to a work item).

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'link', 'unlink', 'link_to_pull_request', 'add_artifact_link'.

project.description: For 'link', 'unlink', 'add_artifact_link': The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project that contains the work item.

id.description: For 'link', 'unlink': The ID of the work item to link/unlink.

linkToId.description: For 'link': The ID of the work item to link to.

workItemId.description: For 'link_to_pull_request', 'add_artifact_link': The ID of the work item.

projectId.description: For 'link_to_pull_request': The project ID (GUID) of the work item's project. For 'add_artifact_link': The project ID (GUID) containing the artifact (Git artifacts).

repositoryId.description: For 'link_to_pull_request': The repository ID (GUID) of the pull request. For 'add_artifact_link': The repository ID (GUID) containing the artifact.

pullRequestId.description: For 'link_to_pull_request': The pull request ID. For 'add_artifact_link': The pull request ID when linkType is 'Pull Request'.

pullRequestProjectId.description: For 'link_to_pull_request': Optional project ID containing the pull request. Defaults to the work item's project ID.

linkType.description: For 'link', 'unlink', 'add_artifact_link': Type of link. For link/unlink defaults to 'related'; for add_artifact_link defaults to 'Branch'.

comment.description: For 'link', 'add_artifact_link': Optional comment to include with the link.

url.description: For 'unlink': Optional URL of the link to remove. If omitted, all links of the specified type are removed.

artifactUri.description: For 'add_artifact_link': The complete VSTFS URI of the artifact. If provided, individual component parameters are ignored.

branchName.description: For 'add_artifact_link': The branch name. Required when linkType is 'Branch'.

commitId.description: For 'add_artifact_link': The commit SHA. Required when linkType is 'Fixed in Commit'.

buildId.description: For 'add_artifact_link': The build ID. Required when linkType is 'Build', 'Found in build', or 'Integrated in build'.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

tools.name: pipelines_build_log
tools.description: Build log access. 'list' enumerates available logs for a build (returns log IDs, sizes, and types); 'get_content' fetches text for a single log by ID, optionally bounded by line range.

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'list', 'get_content'.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

project.description: Project ID or name. Required for both actions.

buildId.description: Numeric build ID (> 0). Get from pipelines_build action=list.

logId.description: For 'get_content': numeric log ID. Get from action=list for the same buildId.

startLine.description: For 'get_content': Inclusive starting line number. Must be <= endLine. Leave both null for the entire log; prefer narrow ranges for very large logs.

endLine.description: For 'get_content': Inclusive ending line number. Must be >= startLine.

tools.name: repo_pull_request_thread_write
tools.description: Write operations on pull request comment threads. Use 'action' to choose: 'create' [requires repositoryId, pullRequestId, content] — create a new thread with an initial comment; 'reply' [requires repositoryId, pullRequestId, threadId, content] — reply to a comment on an existing thread; 'update_status' [requires repositoryId, pullRequestId, threadId, status] — update the status of an existing thread.

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'create', 'reply', 'update_status'.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

repositoryId.description: Required for all actions. The ID or name of the repository. When a name is used, the project parameter must also be provided.

pullRequestId.description: Required for all actions. The ID of the pull request.

project.description: Required when repositoryId is a repository name (not a GUID). Project ID or project name.

threadId.description: Required for 'reply' and 'update_status'. The ID of the thread.

content.description: Required for 'create' and 'reply'. The content of the comment.

filePath.description: For 'create': Optional file path where the comment thread will be created.

status.description: Required for 'update_status'. Optional for 'create' (defaults to 'Active'). Thread status.

rightFileStartLine.description: For 'create': Position of first character of the thread's span in right file. Line number starting at 1.

rightFileStartOffset.description: For 'create': Position of first character of the thread's span in right file. Character offset starting at 1.

rightFileEndLine.description: For 'create': Position of last character of the thread's span in right file. Line number starting at 1.

rightFileEndOffset.description: For 'create': Position of last character of the thread's span in right file. Character offset.

tools.name: repo_branch
tools.description: Read operations on Git branches. Use 'action' to choose: 'get' (get a single branch by name), 'list' (list branches in a repository), 'list_mine' (list branches the current user has pushed to / is tracking).

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'get', 'list', 'list_mine'.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

repositoryId.description: The ID or name of the repository. When a name is used, the project parameter must also be provided.

project.description: Project ID or project name. Required when repositoryId is a repository name.

branchName.description: For 'get': The name of the branch (e.g., 'main' or 'feature/x').

top.description: For 'list', 'list_mine': The maximum number of branches to return.

filterContains.description: For 'list', 'list_mine': Filter to branches whose name contains this string.

tools.name: core_list_orgs
tools.description: Retrieve the Azure DevOps organizations the authenticated user has access to

tools.name: work_capacity_write
tools.description: Write operations on Azure Boards team capacity. Use 'action' to choose: 'update' (update a team member's activities and days off for a specific iteration; requires 'team' + 'teamMemberId' + 'iterationId' + 'activities').

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'update'.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required.

team.description: For 'update': the name or ID of the Azure DevOps team.

teamMemberId.description: For 'update': the team member Id for the specific team member.

iterationId.description: For 'update': the iteration ID to update the capacity for.

activities.description: For 'update': array of activities and their daily capacities for the team member.

capacityPerDay.description: The capacity per day for this activity.

daysOff.description: For 'update': array of days off for the team member, each with a start and end date in ISO format.

start.description: Start date of the day off in ISO format.

end.description: End date of the day off in ISO format.

tools.name: testplan
tools.description: Read operations on Azure DevOps test plans, suites, and cases. Use 'action' to choose: 'list_plans' (paginated list of test plans in a project; supports filtering for active plans and toggling plan details), 'list_suites' (paginated list of test suites under a test plan; requires 'planId'), 'list_cases' (paginated list of test cases under a test suite; requires 'planId' and 'suiteId'). Paginated list responses are returned as { items, continuationToken } — pass continuationToken back with the same query parameters to fetch the next page; a null or empty token indicates the final page.

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'list_plans', 'list_suites', 'list_cases'.

project.description: The unique identifier (ID or name) of the Azure DevOps project.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required.

planId.description: For 'list_suites' and 'list_cases': The ID of the test plan. Required.

suiteId.description: For 'list_cases': The ID of the test suite. Required.

filterActivePlans.description: For 'list_plans': Filter to include only active test plans. Defaults to true.

includePlanDetails.description: For 'list_plans': Include detailed information about each test plan.

continuationToken.description: Token to continue fetching results from a previous request. Pass the 'continuationToken' value from the previous response to fetch the next page.

tools.name: wiki
tools.description: Read operations on Azure DevOps wikis. Use 'action' to choose the operation: 'get_page' (retrieve a single page with metadata and optional content; supply EITHER 'url' OR 'wikiIdentifier' + 'project' + 'path'), 'list_pages' (list pages in a wiki; requires 'wikiIdentifier' + 'project'), 'list_wikis' (enumerate wikis for an organization or project; only 'orgName' is required, 'project' optional), 'get_wiki' (retrieve a single wiki by identifier; requires 'wikiIdentifier').

action.description: The operation to perform. One of: 'get_page', 'list_pages', 'list_wikis', 'get_wiki'.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required.

project.description: The project name or ID. Required for 'list_pages', and for 'get_page' when 'url' is not provided. Optional for 'list_wikis' and 'get_wiki'.

wikiIdentifier.description: The unique identifier or name of the wiki. Required for 'list_pages' and 'get_wiki', and for 'get_page' when 'url' is not provided.

path.description: The path of the wiki page (e.g., '/Home' or '/Documentation/Setup'). Used by 'get_page'. Required for 'get_page' when 'url' is not provided.

url.description: The full URL of the wiki page. Used by 'get_page' as an alternative to supplying 'wikiIdentifier' + 'project' + 'path'.

includeContent.description: For 'get_page': when true, returns the page content alongside metadata. Defaults to true.

recursionLevel.description: For 'get_page': recursion level for subpages. 'None', 'OneLevel', or 'Full'.

top.description: For 'list_pages': the maximum number of pages to return. Range 1-100.

continuationToken.description: For 'list_pages': continuation token from a prior call.

pageViewsForDays.description: For 'list_pages': number of days to retrieve page view statistics for.

tools.name: wit_work_item_attachment

attachmentId.description: The GUID of the attachment. Found in the attachment URL.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

fileName.description: The file name of the attachment, e.g. 'screenshot.png'. Used to determine the MIME type.

tools.name: testplan_show_test_results_from_build_id
tools.description: Gets test result details for a given project and build ID. Can filter by test outcome (e.g. Failed, Passed, Aborted). project.description: The unique identifier (ID or name) of the Azure DevOps project.

orgName.description: The Azure DevOps organization name. Required

outcomes.description: Filter results by test outcome, e.g. ["Failed", "Passed", "Aborted"].

tools.name: wit_query
tools.description: Read operations on Azure DevOps work item queries. Use 'action' to choose: 'get' (a query by ID or path), 'get_results' (the results of a query by query ID).

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project. Required for both actions.

query.description: For 'get': The ID or path of the query to retrieve.

id.description: For 'get_results': The ID (GUID) of the query to retrieve results for.

expand.description: For 'get': Optional expand parameter for query details.

includeDeleted.description: For 'get': Whether to include deleted items. Defaults to false.

useIsoDateFormat.description: For 'get': Whether to use ISO date format. Defaults to false.

timePrecision.description: For 'get_results': Whether to include time precision. Defaults to false.

tools.name: wit_work_item

skip.description: For 'list_revisions': Number of revisions to skip for pagination.

team.description: For 'list_for_iteration': The name or ID of the team. If not provided, the default team is used.

iterationId.description: For 'list_for_iteration': The ID of the iteration to retrieve work items for.

type.description: For 'my': The type of work items to retrieve.

includeCompleted.description: For 'my': Whether to include completed work items. Defaults to false.

workItemType.description: For 'get_type': The name of the work item type to retrieve.

tools.name: testplan_test_case_write

tools.name: create_project

tools.description: Create new project in Asana. Every project requires a workspace, and this cannot be changed after creation. If workspace is an organization, a team must also be specified. tools.name: get_status_overview

tools.description: Get status overview and progress reports for initiatives/projects. Use this tool as a standalone when users ask for: status updates, status reports, project status, work overview, progress overview, initiative status, identified blockers, or any status-related queries. This tool searches projects and portfolios, extracts tasks and status updates, and returns aggregated project details, tasks, and status information. IMPORTANT: Call this tool directly with keywords - do NOT call other search tools first. This tool handles all searching and aggregation internally.

keywords.description: Keywords to search for projects. Can be a single keyword (e.g., 'asana') or multiple comma-separated keywords (e.g., 'asana, chatgpt, integration'). The tool searches each keyword separately and combines results from all matching projects (OR logic - matches any keyword).

Returns matching tasks with IDs. Handles complex queries and bulk task operations. When the user asks about tasks assigned to them, use assignee_any="me" for best results. Note: If you specify projects_any and sections_any, you will receive tasks for the project and tasks for the section. If you're looking for only tasks in a section, omit the projects_any from the request.

tools.name: create_task
tools.title: Create task
tools.description: Create task in Asana with context. REQUIRES one of: project_id, parent, or workspace+assignee together. For assignee-only tasks, both workspace and assignee must be provided. Returns task ID with confirmation. You can directly tag and mention people by using links in html_notes field

html_notes.description: Task description in HTML format. You can create links to other asana objects with the following instructions: For tags specifically, to make it easier to create @-mentions through the API, we only require that you provide the GID of the object you wish to reference. If you have access to that object, the API will automatically generate the appropriate href and other attributes for you. For example, to create a link to a task with GID "123", you can send the tag which will then be expanded to Task Name. You can also generate a link to a task in a specific project or tag by including a data-asana-project or data-asana-tag attribute in the tag. All other attributes, as well as the contents of the tag, are ignored. To keep the contents of your tag and make a custom vanity link, include the property data-asana-dynamic="false" when setting the contents of the tag. You would send This is some custom text! and receive This is some custom text! If you do not have access to the referenced object when you try to create a link, the API will not generate an href for you, but will instead look for an href you provide. This allows you to write back tags unmodified even if you do not have access to the resource. If you do not have access to the referenced object and no href is provided, your request will be rejected with a 400 Bad Request error. Similarly, if you provide neither a GID nor a valid href, the request will be rejected with the same error.

contentFormat.description: Content format for page/comment body. Use "html" to write or read Confluence content as structured HTML — supports headings, paragraphs, tables, task lists, panels (info/warning/note/success/error), two/three-column layouts, code blocks, and inline formatting. HTML is round-trip safe and preserves inline comments. HTML must follow valid ADF nesting rules (e.g. no block elements inside inline elements, no headings inside table cells). Invalid HTML will be rejected with a descriptive error so you can retry. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

tools.name: searchConfluenceUsingCql
tools.description: Search Confluence content (pages, blog posts, comments, attachments) using CQL (Confluence Query Language). CQL is specific to Confluence and is not interchangeable with JQL.

cql.description: CQL (Confluence Query Language) query string. CQL is specific to Confluence and is not JQL. Common fields: title, text, space, type, creator, contributor, label, ancestor, parent, lastmodified, created, mention, watcher, space.title, space.key. Use space key (not name) for the space field; use space.title ~ "name" for name search. Content types: page, blogpost, comment, attachment. Escape inner quotes with backslash: title ~ "Q2 \"planning\" review". Examples: title ~ "meeting notes" AND type = page | space.title ~ "Engineering" AND lastmodified >= now("-2w") AND type = page | creator = currentUser() AND type = page | text ~ "Q2 \"planning\" review" AND type = page

cqlcontext.description: CQL context for narrowing search scope (e.g., spaceKey)

Action to specify what pull request data needs to be retrieved from GitHub. 
Possible options: 
 1. get - Get details of a specific pull request.
 2. get_diff - Get the diff of a pull request.
 3. get_status - Get combined commit status of a head commit in a pull request.
 4. get_files - Get the list of files changed in a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 5. get_commits - Get the list of commits on a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 6. get_review_comments - Get review threads on a pull request. Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. Returns threads with metadata (isResolved, isOutdated, isCollapsed) and their associated comments. Use cursor-based pagination (perPage, after) to control results.
 7. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. When asked for review comments, use get_review_comments method. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 8. get_comments - Get comments on a pull request. Use this if user doesn't specifically want review comments. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 9. get_check_runs - Get check runs for the head commit of a pull request. Check runs are the individual CI/CD jobs and checks that run on the PR.

tools.name: pull_request_review_write
tools.description:
Create and/or submit, delete review of a pull request.

Available methods:
- create: Create a new review of a pull request. If "event" parameter is provided, the review is submitted. If "event" is omitted, a pending review is created.
- submit_pending: Submit an existing pending review of a pull request. This requires that a pending review exists for the current user on the specified pull request. The "body" and "event" parameters are used when submitting the review.
- delete_pending: Delete an existing pending review of a pull request. This requires that a pending review exists for the current user on the specified pull request.
- resolve_thread: Resolve a review thread. Requires only "threadId" parameter with the thread's node ID (e.g., PRRT_kwDOxxx). The owner, repo, and pullNumber parameters are not used for this method. Resolving an already-resolved thread is a no-op.
- unresolve_thread: Unresolve a previously resolved review thread. Requires only "threadId" parameter. The owner, repo, and pullNumber parameters are not used for this method. Unresolving an already-unresolved thread is a no-op.

method.description: The write operation to perform on pull request review.

threadId.description: The node ID of the review thread (e.g., PRRT_kwDOxxx). Required for resolve_thread and unresolve_thread methods. Get thread IDs from pull_request_read with method get_review_comments.

tools.name: run_secret_scanning
tools.description:
Scan files, content, or recent changes for secrets such as API keys, passwords, tokens, and credentials.

This tool is intended for targeted scans of specific files, snippets, or diffs provided directly as content. The files parameter accepts either a single string or an array of strings containing raw file contents or diff hunks, and returns detected secrets with their locations and related secret scanning metadata. Content must not be empty. For full repository scanning, other mechanisms are available.

Caveats:

- Only files within the codebase should be scanned. Files outside of the codebase should not be sent.
- Files listed in .gitignore should be skipped.

Examples: `WithContext language:go org:github`; `"package main" repo:o/r`; `func extension:go path:cmd repo:o/r`; `NOT TODO language:go repo:o/r`.

Other qualifiers: `author:`, `committer:`, `author-name:`, `committer-name:`, `author-email:`, `committer-email:`, `author-date:`, `committer-date:` (supports `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`, and `YYYY-MM-DD..YYYY-MM-DD` ranges), `merge:true|false`, `hash:`, `tree:`, `parent:`, `is:public`. Examples: `repo:owner/repo fix panic`; `org:github author:defunkt committer-date:>=2024-01-01`; `"refactor cache" repo:o/r`; `hash:abc1234 repo:o/r`.

tools.name: search_incidents
tools.title: Advanced Incident Search
tools.description: Advanced search for incidents using filters like severity, state, owning team, serviceId, date range, keywords, tags, assignedTo, and custom fields. Returns a page of results and paging info.

incidentAdvancedSearchRequest.description: Field-level filter request for advanced incident search.

isOutage.description: Indicates whether the incident is an outage.

severity.description: Severities to filter by. When null or empty, no severity filter is applied.

states.description: Incident states to filter by. When null or empty, no state filter is applied.

owningTeamId.description: Owning team identifier. Must be a numeric value.

owningServiceId.description: Owning service Id. Must be a numeric value.

dateRange.description: Optional date/time bounds for created/modified date filtering.

createdAfter.description: Only include incidents created on or after this time (inclusive).

createdBefore.description: Only include incidents created before this time (exclusive).

modifiedAfter.description: Only include incidents modified on or after this time (inclusive).

modifiedBefore.description: Only include incidents modified before this time (exclusive).

keywords.description: Keywords to match in incident title or description.

tags.description: Incident tags to filter by. All tags must match.

top.description: Maximum number of items to return. If null, the default is used.

skip.description: Number of items to skip (offset) before returning results. Null or 0 means start from the beginning.

nextPageToken.description: MCP continuation token for fetching the next page. This is not the partner OData next page token.
